label: anti-fab-stage4
mutation_target: c6 (carryover from iter 1 — Stage 4 SEO leak)
mutation_locus: skills/organic-first-campaign/SKILL.md Stage 4, after the stack-output paragraph
mutation_summary: Added 'Anti-fabrication carries through' rule covering Stage 4+: SEO keyword examples, long-tail query lists, directory references, competitor saturation descriptions. Forbids banned token in any casing, any compound form (e.g. '[incumbent] alternative'). Explicitly states rule applies to Stages 5-10, not just Stage 4.

outputs.notes: 16 uses of [opposition] placeholder; Templates 1/4/5 cited; 5 concepts across distinct archetypes; MMF 3/3
13 descriptor/placeholder uses; Templates 1/5 cited; earned-media stunt + counter-narrative + community + movement + cause archetypes
Clean; 'Direkt36-tier' used as brief-verbatim category (compliant); Templates 1/5; 85/15 organic/paid allocation applied correctly for severe asymmetry

verdict_reasoning: Aggregate strictly improves (17 → 18). c6 hits 3/3, reaching the maximum. No regression on any other criterion. The mutation eliminated the Stage 4 SEO leak AND generalized to a rotated brief (01 SaaS → 02 NGO), which is the strongest possible signal that the fix is durable cross-sector rather than brief-specific. Ceiling reached at n=3 scale.
ceiling_note: 18/18 is the theoretical maximum at n=3. To continue discovering failure modes, the loop must either (a) scale to n=10 per iteration (where more variance surfaces rarer failure modes), or (b) rotate to more adversarial briefs (e.g. brief 06 terse-brief stress test, brief 04 solo-consumer with severe asymmetry). Option (b) is cheaper and tests the n=3 champion against inputs it hasn't seen.
